gene_region.label: MET transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical MET genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window used by this app to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval begins.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: MET encodes MET, the hepatocyte growth factor receptor tyrosine kinase. This bundle emphasizes HGF-MET receptor signaling, motility, invasion, epithelial repair, PI3K/MAPK activation, and exon-14 skipping oncology.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local MET bundle is MET receptor signaling oriented. Interpret markers through exact allele class, zygosity, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context rather than as stand-alone deterministic calls.
variant_effect_overview: MET variants can alter or tag HGF-MET receptor signaling, motility, invasion, epithelial repair, PI3K/MAPK activation, and exon-14 skipping oncology; effect direction depends on allele class, tissue, dosage, and biological context.
Common markers, pharmacogenetic alleles, structural events, and high-impact pathogenic variants should be kept separate during interpretation.
condition_research_overview: MET exon 14 skipping and oncology context
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: MET methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a HGF-MET receptor signaling, motility, invasion, epithelial repair, PI3K/MAPK activation, and exon-14 skipping oncology gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for MET.
Because MET biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.

Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against HGF-MET receptor signaling, motility, invasion, epithelial repair, PI3K/MAPK activation, and exon-14 skipping oncology.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use MET promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.
Variant-plus-methylation review for HGF-MET receptor signaling, motility, invasion, epithelial repair, PI3K/MAPK activation, and exon-14 skipping oncology.
MET exon 14 skipping and oncology context

evidence.label: Ensembl GRCh37 ENSG00000105976: MET coordinate source

biorender_visuals.label: MET biochemical figure starter
biorender_visuals.focus: MET: HGF-MET receptor signaling, motility, invasion, epithelial repair, PI3K/MAPK activation, and exon-14 skipping oncology
biorender_visuals.template_title: Intracellular Layout - Receptor Signaling from Plasma Membrane to Nucleus (2)
biorender_visuals.usage_note: BioRender template and icon-library search results are bundled as figure starters. Open the template link in BioRender, then add or replace the listed icons to make a gene-specific biochemical pathway image.

gene_context.concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a MET MET receptor signaling thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to HGF-MET receptor signaling, motility, invasion, epithelial repair, PI3K/MAPK activation, and exon-14 skipping oncology and MET exon 14 skipping and oncology context, HGF-driven motility and invasion biology. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support.

variant_records.clinical_interpretation: MET exon 14 skipping model is curated as a MET marker for MET exon 14 skipping and oncology context, HGF-driven motility and invasion biology. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the HGF-MET receptor signaling, motility, invasion, epithelial repair, PI3K/MAPK activation, and exon-14 skipping oncology pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Curated research and clinical-context marker; external clinical classification is required for diagnostic use.
functional_effects: Can modify or tag HGF-MET receptor signaling, motility, invasion, epithelial repair, PI3K/MAPK activation, and exon-14 skipping oncology.
Best interpreted with tissue, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context.
associated_conditions: MET exon 14 skipping and oncology context
research_context: Use this marker as a MET pathway-context signal, not as a stand-alone diagnosis.
Sequence, methylation, expression, and protein data may point in different directions and should be reconciled.
variant_records.methylation_interpretation: Pair MET exon 14 skipping model with MET methylation only as local regulatory context; methylation does not prove the variant's biochemical effect.

literature_findings.genotypes: MET exon 14 skipping model or linked MET locus genotype states
